turns.user_message: I’ve been noticing lately that I catch myself apologizing for things that aren’t even my fault. It just started a few weeks ago, and it feels really strange. I’ll say sorry for the weather or for someone else bumping into me, and then I feel this heavy knot in my stomach. I keep wondering why I’m suddenly so quick to take the blame for everything around me. It’s exhausting to constantly feel like I’m taking up space I don’t deserve.

It’s not just little things anymore because I’m starting to assume I’m the reason everything goes sideways. I replay every conversation in my head and convince myself I said the wrong tone or ruined the mood. I used to brush off my mistakes, but now I feel like I’m carrying around this invisible weight of disappointment. Every time someone sighs or looks away, I immediately assume it’s my fault. I hate how quickly I’m turning into someone who expects to be the problem in every room.

I can’t even look at myself in the mirror without feeling this deep, burning sense that I’m fundamentally broken. I’ve convinced myself that my mere presence is a burden that everyone else has to quietly tolerate. Every small misstep proves that I’m just a walking mistake who ruins whatever good things happen to cross my path. I don’t know how to stop apologizing for existing or how to shake this heavy shame that clings to every single breath I take. I just feel completely hollowed out by my own constant failures.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This just started recently.
